source: FDA 2024 ADC Guidance; Saber et al. 2015; Doronina et al. 2016; brentuximab vedotin BLA review
description: MMAE (monomethyl auristatin E) is a membrane-permeable microtubule inhibitor with bystander effect. Primary DLTs: neutropenia and peripheral neuropathy. Used in: brentuximab vedotin, polatuzumab vedotin, enfortumab vedotin, tisotumab vedotin.

expected_findings.typical_magnitude: 30-60% nadir from baseline; nadir Day 7-14, recovery by Day 21-28
expected_findings.severity_threshold: ANC nadir <1.0 x 10^9/L with prolonged duration (>14d) or febrile neutropenia = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Myeloid progenitor cytotoxicity via microtubule disruption during mitosis. Nadir timing predictable; cyclic pattern with dosing schedule.
expected_findings.linker_note: Worsened by premature vc-linker cleavage. Rat vc-PABC linker cleaved by serine proteases — 5-66x higher free MMAE in rat vs cyno. Rat findings may overstate human systemic payload exposure.
expected_findings.pk_correlate: Free MMAE AUC + intact ADC AUC

expected_findings.severity_threshold: >50% cellularity reduction or multilineage involvement or absent megakaryocytes = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Direct cytotoxicity of hematopoietic progenitors via microtubule disruption. Histopathological correlate of peripheral cytopenias.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 1 (minimal axonal degeneration, functionally silent) is expected. Grade 2+ with proprioceptive deficits, NCV reduction, or DRG neuronal necrosis = adverse. CRITICAL: preclinical Grade 1 may translate to clinical Grade 2-3 — known MMAE translation gap.
expected_findings.rationale: Microtubule disruption in non-dividing peripheral neurons; axonal transport failure; cumulative with repeat dosing. Monitor NF-L as biomarker.
expected_findings.translation_gap: Cyno Grade 1 axonal degeneration may translate to clinical Grade 2-3 peripheral neuropathy. Dosing regimen difference (cyno q3w vs clinical q3w with lower mg/kg) partially explains gap.
expected_findings.pk_correlate: Free MMAE AUC (peripheral nerve tissue accumulation)

expected_findings.typical_magnitude: ALT up to 3x ULN; mild hepatocyte vacuolation
expected_findings.severity_threshold: ALT >3x ULN with hepatocyte necrosis = adverse. >5x ULN = never reclassify.
expected_findings.rationale: Free MMAE entry into hepatocytes via passive diffusion; microtubule disruption in metabolically active hepatocytes.

expected_findings.description: GI mucosal atrophy / decreased villous height
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Diffuse villous blunting with hemorrhage or diarrhea with clinical weight loss = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Cytotoxicity of rapidly dividing intestinal crypt cells; bystander effect from free MMAE.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Complete alopecia or skin ulceration = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Cycling follicular keratinocytes sensitive to microtubule disruption. Marker of systemic cytotoxic exposure.

expected_findings.description: Thrombocytopenia (mild — MMAE is less thrombocytopenic than MMAF or calicheamicin)
expected_findings.severity_threshold: PLAT <50% baseline with hemorrhage, petechiae, or purpura = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Megakaryocyte inhibition (less potent than MMAF due to membrane permeability difference). Less prominent than neutropenia for MMAE.
